outcome_text: Organizational policies and practices are in place to foster a critical thinking and safety-first mindset in the design, development, deployment, and uses of AI systems to minimize potential negative impacts.
scoring_text: Skill in cultivating organizational AI safety culture, including embedding critical thinking practices into AI development workflows, establishing safety-first design principles, and creating organizational norms that prioritize harm prevention in AI system design, development, deployment, and use.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in cultivating organizational AI safety culture, including embedding critical thinking practices into AI development workflows, establishing safety-first design principles, and creating organizational norms that prioritize harm prevention in AI system design, development, deployment, and use.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI governance structures, including model risk management, AI lifecycle oversight, accountability assignment, and the organizational conditions that enable responsible AI deployment at scale.
comments: NIST AI RMF GV-4.1 (GOVERN) superset of WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-002 is the strongest match (STS: 0.593, cosine: 0.632). Secondary: AG-S-005 (AG, cosine: 0.624), AI-S-010 (AI, cosine: 0.617).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.9/10 (raw 0.593). Shared vocabulary: [ai, deployment, organizational]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [creating, critical, cultivating, culture, design, development] vs WIDAI terms [accountability, assignment, conditions, enable, governance, knowledge] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.890 (P=0.883, R=0.897). Balanced P/R — the 36-word competency implication and 25-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (96.0%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.632 ≈ STS 0.593 (gap 0.039). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, deployment, organizational] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 13.0%. Shared tokens: [ai, deployment, organizational]. NIST AI RMF-only: [creating, critical, cultivating, culture, design, development]. WIDAI-only: [accountability, assignment, conditions, enable, governance, knowledge].
